metadata.raw_text:
Step 1: Customer provides part numbers, PDFs, and Excel sheets.

Step 2: Data extraction and transformation (ETL).

Step 3: Supplier identification from a database.

Step 4: Send RFQ to suppliers via email.

Step 5: Suppliers respond with quotes.

Step 6: Compare supplier quotes using a pricing template.

Step 7: Shortlist and select suppliers based on the comparison.

Step 8: Finalize supplier and confirm order.



end-to-end RFQ automation in LangGraph with two distinct workflows:
1.	Inbound RFQ Workflow (steps 1–4) – handle customer RFQ input, normalize it, enrich with supplier mapping, send out RFQs.
2.	Quotation Processing Workflow (steps 5–8) – handle supplier replies asynchronously, parse them, compare quotes, shortlist, finalize.




Inbound RFQ Workflow (Steps 1–4)
Step 1 – RFQ Ingestion
Objective: Accept RFQ in any format (email body, Excel attachment, PDF, scanned document).
Flow:
1.	Source Identification
o	System receives an RFQ request through email, portal upload, or API.
o	Detects type of input (Email text, Excel sheet, PDF/Scanned PDF).
2.	Extraction per Source
o	Email: Extract inline text (items, notes).
o	Excel: Read tabular data (Item, Description, Qty, UOM, etc.).
o	PDF/Scanned PDF: Apply OCR + text parsing.
3.	Temporary Storage
o	Raw content stored in state with metadata (source_type, raw_text or raw_table).
o	Example: [{source: "excel", content: <rows>}, {source: "pdf", content: <text>}].
________________________________________
Step 2 – Data Extraction & Transformation (ETL)
Objective: Turn raw content into structured, standardized item details.
Flow:
1.	Extraction (via LLM/OCR + parsing rules):
o	Identify mandatory fields:
	Item / Part Number
	Description
	Quantity
	UOM
o	Extract optional fields if available:
	Make / Manufacturer
	Drawing/spec references
	Notes, guidelines, delivery requirements.
2.	Transformation:
o	Standardize fields (uppercase part numbers, consistent UOM like “NOS”, normalized quantity format).
o	Deduplicate rows if the same part appears multiple times.
o	Validate mandatory fields are present; flag if missing.
3.	Load (Consolidation):
o	Merge all sources (Email + Excel + PDF) into a single structured dataset.
o	Save into an Excel file with defined schema:
Item	Description	Qty	UOM	Make	Part No.	Notes
o	This file acts as the master RFQ sheet.
________________________________________
Step 3 – Supplier Identification
Objective: Map each item to the right supplier(s).
Flow:
1.	Supplier Database Lookup:
o	Query supplier database (items mapped to supplier names, emails, capabilities).
o	Match part number or item description against supplier catalog.
2.	Supplier Mapping:
o	Attach list of potential suppliers per item.
o	Each row in RFQ master sheet updated with:
	Supplier Name(s)
	Supplier Email(s).
3.	Validation:
o	If no suppliers found → escalate for manual mapping.
o	If multiple suppliers found → keep all, flagged for later emailing.
________________________________________
Step 4 – RFQ Email Generation & Dispatch
Objective: Send RFQ to all identified suppliers in a standardized format.
Flow:
1.	Email Content Generation (LLM assisted):
o	Create subject line → “Request for Quotation: [Part Number/Project Ref]”.
o	Create email body → Include introduction, RFQ details, instructions for reply.
o	Attach RFQ master Excel sheet OR embed line items directly in email.
2.	Personalization:
o	Address supplier by name.
o	Filter only items relevant to each supplier into their email.
3.	Dispatch:
o	Send emails through mail server/API.
o	Log each dispatch (supplier, timestamp, items included).
4.	Post-Send Tracking:
o	Mark RFQ status as “Sent” in system.
o	Store a reference (RFQ ID + Supplier mapping) for later linking with responses.
________________________________________
Summary of Flow
•	Step 1 (Ingest): Collect RFQ in any format → store raw data.
•	Step 2 (ETL): Extract structured fields → standardize → consolidate into single RFQ Excel.
•	Step 3 (Supplier Mapping): Match items to suppliers via DB → enrich Excel with supplier info.
•	Step 4 (Send Emails): Generate RFQ email per supplier → dispatch → log for tracking.



Quotation Processing Workflow (Steps 5–8)
________________________________________
Step 5
Objective: Capture and track supplier quotations, mapped to RFQ numbers and item purchase numbers.
Process:
1.	Supplier replies by email with attached digital quotation (Excel, PDF, etc.) including RFQ Number in subject or body.
2.	System extracts response data and maps it to corresponding RFQ + Item Purchase Number.
3.	Store norm
Expected Database Table: SupplierResponses
Column Name	Example Value	Notes
ResponseID (PK)	RSP-001	Unique per supplier response
RFQNumber	RFQ123	Links to original RFQ
ItemPurchaseNo	RFQ123-ITEM001	Unique per item request
SupplierID	SUP-45	Foreign key to Supplier table
Price	100	Standardized currency/unit
LeadTime	14 days	To be normalized later
TermsText	"Payment on receipt"	Raw text before LLM normalization
QuotationFilePath	/quotes/rfq123_supplierA.pdf	For traceability
DateReceived	2025-09-16	Timestamp
________________________________________
Step 6 – Standardization & Pricing Comparison
Objective: Put all responses in a uniform structure for side-by-side comparison.
Process:
1.	Normalize all fields:
o	Convert currency to base currency.
o	Convert lead time into standardized units (e.g., days).
o	Keep original terms text, but also run LLM-based interpretation.
2.	Prepare comparison sheet per item with all suppliers listed.
Excel/DB Table: ItemComparison
ItemPurchaseNo	Supplier	Price (USD)	Lead Time (days)	Terms (Normalized)	Notes
RFQ123-ITEM001	Supplier A	100	14	Advance	“Payment on receipt” → mapped by LLM
RFQ123-ITEM001	Supplier B	105	7	Net 15	“Payment within 15 days” → mapped by LLM
RFQ123-ITEM001	Supplier C	110	10	Net 30	“Standard Net 30”
________________________________________
Step 7 – Supplier Shortlisting (Hybrid Scoring)
Objective: Narrow down suppliers using weighted scoring (rules + LLM).
Process:
1.	Rules Engine:
o	Price Score: Lowest price = 100; others scaled proportionally.
o	Lead Time Score: Fastest lead time = 100; others scaled proportionally.
2.	LLM Normalization:
o	TermsText interpreted into categories:
	"Advance" → 60/100 (less favorable).
	"Net 15" → 80/100.
	"Net 30" → 100/100 (most favorable).
3.	Compute final score:
4.	Score = (PriceScore * 0.5) +
5.	        (LeadTimeScore * 0.3) +
6.	        (TermsScore * 0.2)
7.	Rank suppliers per item.
8.	Shortlist top 1–2 suppliers automatically.
Excel/DB Table: Shortlist
ItemPurchaseNo	Supplier	Price	Lead Time	Terms	PriceScore	LeadTimeScore	TermsScore	FinalScore	Rank
RFQ123-ITEM001	Supplier A	100	14	Advance	100	50	60	82	1
RFQ123-ITEM001	Supplier B	105	7	Net 15	95	100	80	93	2
RFQ123-ITEM001	Supplier C	110	10	Net 30	91	70	100	88	3
________________________________________
Step 8 – Final Decision & Order Confirmation
Objective: Select the best supplier and issue Purchase Order (PO).
Process:
1.	Automatically pick Rank 1 supplier per item (or allow configurable logic: e.g., prefer Net 30 even if slightly higher price).
2.	Generate PO including:
o	RFQ Number
o	Item Purchase No
o	Supplier details
o	Agreed price, lead time, terms
3.	Update database with final supplier decision.
4.	Send PO via email automatically.
Database Table: PurchaseOrders
WEIGHT	RFQNumber	ItemPurchaseNo	SupplierID	FinalPrice	FinalLeadTime	Final Terms	POFilePath	DateIssued
PO-001	RFQ123	RFQ123-ITEM001	SUP-45	100	14	Advance	/POs/PO001.pdf	2025-09-16
________________________________________
End-to-End View (Steps 5–8)
1.	Supplier Response Capture → Store in SupplierResponses.
2.	Standardize/Compare → Build ItemComparison sheets.
3.	Shortlisting (Hybrid) → Score & rank into Shortlist.
4.	Final Selection & PO → Confirm into PurchaseOrders.








Expected database Schema :
📐 Master Database Schema for RFQ + Quotation Workflow
________________________________________
1. With
Holds customer details (who sends RFQs).
Column Name	Type	Example	Notes
Customer ID (PK)	INT	CUST-001	Unique customer identifier
Name	VARCHAR	ABC Industries	Company name
ContactPerson	VARCHAR	John Doe	Main contact
Email	VARCHAR	john@abc.com	Primary email
Phone	VARCHAR	+1-555-1234	Contact phone
Address	VARCHAR	New York, USA	Optional
________________________________________
2. RFQ
Each RFQ submitted by a customer.
Column Name	Type	Example	Notes
RFQNumber (PK)	VARCHAR	RFQ123	Unique per RFQ
Customer ID (FK)	INT	CUST-001	Links to Customers
RFQDate	DATE	2025-09-16	Date received
SourceType	ENUM	PDF/Excel/Email	Format of input
FilePath	TEXT	/rfqs/rfq123.pdf	Original document
Status	ENUM	Pending/Processed/Closed	Lifecycle status
________________________________________
3. RFQ
Each part/item requested within an RFQ.
Column Name	Type	Example	Notes
ItemPurchaseNo (PK)	VARCHAR	RFQ123-ITEM001	Unique item reference
RFQNumber (FK)	VARCHAR	RFQ123	Links to RFQ
Part Number	VARCHAR	AEHS636NS	Customer part number
Description	TEXT	Male NATO Plug 28V	Product description
Quantity	INT	2	Requested qty
UOM	VARCHAR	us	Unit of Measure
Specs	TEXT	"28V, 300A, 4 pins"	Technical specs
________________________________________
4. Supplier Table
Master database of suppliers.
Column Name	Type	Example	Notes
SupplierID (PK)	INT	SUP-45	Unique supplier ID
Name	VARCHAR	Anand Enterprises	Supplier company
ContactPerson	VARCHAR	Mr. Kumar	Supplier contact
Email	VARCHAR	sales@anand.com	For sending RFQs
Phone	VARCHAR	+91-22-3333	Optional
Address	TEXT	Mumbai, India	-
Categories	TEXT	Electrical Parts	Classification
________________________________________
5. SupplierMapping Table
Mapping which suppliers can supply which items.
Column Name	Type	Example	Notes
MappingID (PK)	INT	1001	-
Part Number	VARCHAR	AEHS636NS	Item part number
SupplierID (FK)	INT	SUP-45	Supplier who can supply it
________________________________________
6. OutboundRFQ Table (RFQs Sent to Suppliers)
Tracks each supplier email sent for each item.
Column Name	Type	Example	Notes
OutboundRFQID (PK)	INT	ORFQ-001	Unique
RFQNumber (FK)	VARCHAR	RFQ123	Link to RFQ
ItemPurchaseNo (FK)	VARCHAR	RFQ123-ITEM001	Line item
SupplierID (FK)	INT	SUP-45	Target supplier
EmailSent	BOOLEAN	TRUE	Whether sent
DateSent	DATE	2025-09-16	-
Status	ENUM	Sent/Responded/Closed	Track lifecycle
________________________________________
7. SupplierResponses Table (Step 5)
Column Name	Type	Example	Notes
ResponseID (PK)	INT	RSP-001	Unique
OutboundRFQID (FK)	INT	ORFQ-001	Link to supplier RFQ
SupplierID (FK)	INT	SUP-45	Responding supplier
ItemPurchaseNo (FK)	VARCHAR	RFQ123-ITEM001	Line item
Price	DECIMAL	100.00	Normalized
LeadTimeDays	INT	14	Standardized
TermsRaw	TEXT	"Payment on receipt"	Original
TermsNormalized	VARCHAR	Advance/Net30	From LLM
DateReceived	DATE	2025-09-16	-
FilePath	TEXT	/responses/rfq123_supplierA.pdf	-
________________________________________
8. ItemComparison View (Step 6)
(Not a table, but a database view that joins RFQItems + SupplierResponses for easy analysis.)
________________________________________
9. Your Shortlist
Column Name	Type	Example	Notes
ShortlistID (PK)	INT	SL-001	-
ItemPurchaseNo (FK)	VARCHAR	RFQ123-ITEM001	Line item
SupplierID (FK)	INT	SUP-45	Supplier
PriceScore	INT	100	Weighted
LeadTimeScore	INT	50	Weighted
TermsScore	INT	60	Weighted
FinalScore	INT	82	Weighted sum
Rank	INT	1	Position
DateShortlisted	DATE	2025-09-17	-
________________________________________
10. PurchaseOrders Table (Step 8)
Column Name	Type	Example	Notes
WEIGHT (PK)	VARCHAR	PO-001	Unique
RFQNumber (FK)	VARCHAR	RFQ123	Which RFQ
ItemPurchaseNo (FK)	VARCHAR	RFQ123-ITEM001	Which item
SupplierID (FK)	INT	SUP-45	Final supplier
FinalPrice	DECIMAL	100.00	Negotiated/confirmed
FinalLeadTime	INT	14	Confirmed
Final Terms	VARCHAR	Advance	Confirmed
POFilePath	TEXT	/POs/PO001.pdf	Digital PO
DateIssued	DATE	2025-09-17	-
________________________________________
🔗 Relationships (ER-st
•	Customers → (1:M) → RFQs
•	RFQs → (1:M) → RFQItems
•	RFQItems → (M:M via SupplierMapping) → Supplie
•	OutboundRFQ → lRFQItems + Suppliers
•	SupplierResponses → (M:1) → OutboundRFQ
•	SupplierResponses → feeds into Shortlist
•	Shortlist → (1:M) → PurchaseOrders (final selection)
